mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal expanding household income and aggregate demand, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive credit conditions elevate corporate borrowing costs and reduce equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate broad final-demand strength rather than inventory accumulation, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Months where both INDPRO and RSAFS increase simultaneously are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, when unemployment is already low, additional month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate signal accelerating wage pressures that will compress corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE below 5.5% and declining are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving operational leverage and capacity utilization, which boosts profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months where INDPRO rises more than 0.5% are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payrolls combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate indicate a Goldilocks scenario of growth without restrictive policy, generating strongly positive S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with labor market improvement
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising PAYEMS and flat-or-falling FEDFUNDS fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below one million units signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with HOUST below 1000 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices above a threshold signals inflationary pressure that reduces real corporate earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Months where CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.25% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.
